isolation_opt_out_reason: Rollup feature that aggregates 23 small/medium audit-driven enhancements shipping as individual short-lived branches off main (per the 2026-05-08 fitme-story public-site audit). The rollup state.json itself is metadata-only; per-sub-task PRs follow standard branch isolation (each on its own feature/* branch). Today's 7 shipped sub-tasks landed via PR #59 (a-c) + PR #61 (g) following the standard convention. Mode C of BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION is opted out to allow this state.json to be updated from main when only documentation/scope changes accumulate (no code mutations live here).

research.reason: work_type:enhancement; B_medium tier latitude (per F6 v7.9 candidate). Audit synthesis at docs/research/2026-05-08-fitme-story-audit-synthesis.md serves as the research-equivalent — 3,100-word broad audit + 28-finding case-study deep-dive + 6-P0 / 12-quick-wins synthesis.

prd.reason: work_type:enhancement; PRD-equivalent lives in the audit synthesis (success metrics + scope + sequencing) and the website-enhancement-queue spec (per-item scope + class). Per-sub-task PRs carry their own scope statements in PR description.
prd.analytics_spec_note: No new analytics events — enhancements are mechanical (a11y, perf, schema, layout). Re-evaluate when SEARCH-1 ships (search analytics may warrant new events).

tasks.approved_note: 23 tasks: 17 done (T1-T7 via PR #59 + #61; T8/T11/T14/T15/T16/T17/T21 reconciled 2026-05-08 evening; T9 via PR #67; T22+T23 via PR #258 v7.8.2 documented exemption), 6 pending (T10 site-wide search, T12 35 Figma frames, T13 v7.9 mirror gated 2026-05-21, T18-T20 Figma file + tokens + Code Connect). See tasks.md for the full breakdown.

ux_or_integration.reason: work_type:enhancement; per-sub-task UX dispatched in-line. Most items are mechanical (a11y, perf, schema, MDX pipeline). Larger UI items (P-MOBNAV, P-CALLOUTS, ArticleNav) dispatch /ux + /design when picked up — T7 (ArticleNav) already shipped this way via PR #61 with the audit synthesis serving as ux-spec equivalent.

implementation.commits_note: Squash commits on main from this rollup: 95cb4d1 (PR #59 T1-T6 quick-wins), a548e5f (PR #61 T7 ArticleNav), d560500 (PR #62 T16 MobileNav), ef1077f (PR #65 T17 rehype-pretty-code+CopyButton), 3da5591 (PR #66 T15 Callouts), 83b1a89 (PR #63 T11+T14 buildMetadata+SEO), e79bc8c (PR #67 T9 frontmatter audit), f252ded (FT2 PR #254 audit synthesis docs), 2975e74 (FT2 PR #260 T8 dual-outlet), 3176038 (FT2 PR #261 T21 architecture doc). 15 of 23 tasks shipped via 9 squash PRs.

testing.testing_note: Per-sub-task: each PR runs Vercel preview build + GitGuardian + verify CI. All 3 today-PRs (#59, #61, FT2 #254) shipped CI-green. No new dedicated unit tests added (mechanical changes; no new business logic to assert). Future tasks with logic (SEARCH-1, P-CALLOUTS) will add tests.

review.review_note: Per-sub-task code review handled in PR. No formal pre-merge-review dispatched for this rollup itself (each PR carries its own).

merge.pr_number_note: This rollup feature does not have a single merge PR. Each sub-task ships as its own PR. See tasks[].pr_number for the per-task PR list. Today's 7 done items shipped via fitme-story PR #59 (T1-T6) + PR #61 (T7) + FT2 PR #254 (audit docs).

documentation.documentation_note: Audit synthesis + website-enhancement-queue spec written ahead of work (FT2 PR #254). Per-sub-task PR descriptions document what landed. Final case study at feature closure.

tasks.title: Skip-to-content link + WCAG contrast bump (light-mode --color-neutral-500)
aria-current + 2px underline on nav + global focus-visible ring + back-to-catalog with copy-link toolbar
rehype-slug + rehype-autolink-headings — every heading becomes a deep-link target
.prose table display:block + overflow-x:auto — fixes mobile table overflow corpus-wide

tasks.scope_note: Stress-test S3-G3 deferred 2026-05-07. Document the contract: what belongs in FT2 source case study (long-form, technical, append-only journal) vs fitme-story slot MDX (short-form, public showcase). Lives at docs/case-studies/dual-outlet-pattern.md. Blocks G5 (T9) since G5 needs G3's contract to know what 'complete frontmatter' means.

tasks.title: Timeline frontmatter audit — backfill consistency across 47 showcase MDX files
tasks.scope_note: Stress-test S3-G5 deferred 2026-05-07. Walk every showcase MDX in fitme-story/content/04-case-studies/ (47 files); verify each carries v7.7+ standard frontmatter (date, version, work_type, success_metrics, kill_criteria, kill_criterion_fired, dispatch_pattern, etc.). Audit synthesis appendix already shows the corpus-wide gap: 5 missing honest_disclosures, 6 missing visual_aid, 27 missing kill_criteria. Backfill OR set chrome_minimal: true with documented reason (T5's schema).

tasks.title: Site-wide keyword search with smart ranked results across case-studies + glossary + dev-guide + lifecycle-event-catalog
tasks.scope_note: Persistent header search + /search?q= page. Tag-aware ranking (frontmatter matches > body matches). Faceted filters (version / work_type / era / tier). Pagefind recommended (~80 docs ≈ tiny bundle). Benefits from G3+G5 done first so tag facets are accurate. Already on backlog as formal entry (line 166).

cache_hits.task_context: Reused audit-driven enhancement pattern from v7.7 validity closure (broad audit → synthesis → quick-wins bundle → strategic recommendations → per-PR ship). Pattern adapted to public-site context.
Reused rehype-slug + rehype-autolink-headings pattern from generic Next.js+MDX best practices (well-known plugin install). Saved Phase 0 research.
Reused PR description template + test plan checklist style + cross-reference pattern from yesterday's ucc-passkey-auth PRs. Saved drafting time on PR #59 + #61 bodies.

cu_v2.rationale: Rollup of 23 small/medium audit-driven enhancements. Net new code is small and surgical per item. Complexity comes from breadth (10+ sub-PRs over time) not depth. Blast radius medium because changes are user-facing on every showcase reader. Novelty low (well-trodden a11y/perf/SEO patterns). Verification medium (mostly visual + Vercel preview spot-checks; eval-gate not applicable since no AI behaviors).
